title: Use the event_loop fixture
body:
Refs:
- https://github.com/simonw/datasette/issues/2461#issuecomment-2634920351

<!-- readthedocs-preview datasette start -->
----
📚 Documentation preview 📚: https://datasette--2463.org.readthedocs.build/en/2463/

<!-- readthedocs-preview datasette end -->

step_plan.summary: Add a session-scoped pytest event_loop fixture to tests/conftest.py so pytest-asyncio and async fixtures/tests use a consistent asyncio event loop, avoiding platform-specific differences and warnings.

steps.operation: Add a session-scoped pytest event_loop fixture
steps.reason: pytest-asyncio looks for an event_loop fixture to provide a consistent asyncio event loop to async tests and fixtures. Adding a session-scoped event_loop that creates a new loop, sets it as the current loop, yields it for tests, then closes and clears it at teardown ensures deterministic behavior across platforms (avoids Proactor/Selector differences on Windows), removes runtime warnings, and makes async fixtures (e.g. ds_client) reliable.
steps.side_effects: Tests will run against a single session-scoped event loop which improves determinism and removes platform-specific warnings. Some tests or fixtures that previously relied on implicit global loop lifetimes or on creating/closing loops per-function may need adjustments. No public API changes; only test behavior changes. Downstream dependent test utilities that assume different loop scope may require updates. Ensure pytest-asyncio remains installed/configured; running tests may reveal fixtures that need function-scoped loops instead of session-scoped ones.
